text: {"claims":[{"statement":"Omega-3 fatty acids can help reduce blood levels of triglycerides, a known risk factor for heart disease.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Omega-3 fatty acids [can helpTrusted Source](https://www.ahajournals.org/doi/full/10.1161/CIR.0000000000000709) reduce blood levels of triglycerides, a known risk factor for heart disease.","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly states that omega‑3 fatty acids reduce blood triglyceride levels, which are a risk factor for heart disease.","population":"","intervention":"Omega-3 fatty acids","comparator":"","outcome":"blood triglyceride levels","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Consuming omega-3-enriched eggs can effectively help lower blood triglycerides.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Studies suggest consuming omega-3-enriched eggs can effectively help lower blood triglycerides.","entailment":"The quote reports that studies suggest omega‑3‑enriched eggs lower triglycerides, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"omega-3-enriched eggs","comparator":"","outcome":"blood triglycerides","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"A small 2020 study of 20 participants found eating 2 omega-3-enriched eggs daily for five weeks reduced triglycerides by 10%.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"A [small 2020 study](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7300047/) of 20 participants found eating 2 omega-3-enriched eggs daily for five weeks reduced triglycerides by 10%.","entailment":"The quoted sentence provides the study details and the observed 10% reduction in triglycerides, directly supporting the claim.","population":"20 participants","intervention":"2 omega-3-enriched eggs daily","comparator":"","outcome":"triglycerides","timeframe":"five weeks","quantifiers":"10% reduction","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Additional research with larger groups of participants is still needed.","claimType":"methodological","quote":"However, additional research with larger groups of participants is still needed.","entailment":"The quote explicitly states that further larger‑scale research is required, supporting the claim about the need for more studies.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Proteins are the main building blocks of the human body.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"Proteins are the main building blocks of the human body.","entailment":"The quoted sentence defines proteins as the primary structural components of the body, matching the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"It is important to get enough protein in the diet, and research suggests that the currently recommended amounts may be too low.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"It is important to get [enough protein](https://www.healthline.com/nutrition/how-much-protein-per-day) in the diet, and [research](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9963165/) suggests that the currently recommended amounts may be too low.","entailment":"The quote conveys both the importance of adequate protein intake and that research indicates current recommendations might be insufficient, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"adequate protein intake","comparator":"current recommended amounts","outcome":"protein adequacy","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Eggs are an excellent source of protein, with a single large egg containing six grams of it.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Eggs are an excellent source of protein, with a single large egg containing six grams of it.","entailment":"The quoted sentence provides the quantitative protein content of a large egg, confirming the claim about eggs being a good protein source.","population":"","intervention":"single large egg","comparator":"","outcome":"protein (6 g)","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"6 g","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Eggs also contain all the essential amino acids in the right ratios, so your body is well‑equipped to make full use of the protein in them.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"Eggs also contain all the essential amino acids in the right ratios, so your body is well-equipped to make full use of the protein in them.","entailment":"The quote states that eggs have a complete essential amino acid profile in appropriate ratios, supporting the claim about their nutritional completeness.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"Eating enough protein may help with weight loss.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Eating enough protein may help with:\n- [weight loss](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8468854/)","entailment":"The quoted bullet point links sufficient protein intake to potential weight‑loss benefits, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"adequate protein intake","comparator":"","outcome":"weight loss","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Eggs are incredibly filling.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Eggs are incredibly filling.","entailment":"The quote directly describes eggs as highly satiating, supporting the claim about their filling nature.","population":"","intervention":"eggs consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"satiety","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Eggs score high on a satiety index, which measures foods’ ability to cause feelings of fullness and reduce later calorie intake, according to a 1995 study that developed the index.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Eggs score high on a scale called the satiety index, which measures foods’ ability to cause feelings of fullness and reduce later calorie intake, according to a [1995 studyTrusted Source](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/7498104/) that developed the index.","entailment":"The quoted sentence reports that eggs rank highly on the satiety index, indicating strong fullness‑inducing properties, as established by the cited study.","population":"","intervention":"eggs consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"satiety index score","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"In one study of 50 overweight or obese adults, eating eggs and toast instead of cereal and milk with orange juice decreased feelings of hunger, prolonged the period of not being hungry and made them eat about 180 calories less at lunch four hours later.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"In one [study](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7432073/) of 50 adults who were overweight or had obesity, eating eggs and toast instead of cereal and milk with orange juice decreased feelings of hunger following the meal, prolonged the period of not being hungry and made them eat ~180 calories less at lunch 4 hours later.","entailment":"The quote details the study design, participants, and observed outcomes (reduced hunger and ~180 kcal lower lunch intake), directly supporting the claim.","population":"50 overweight or obese adults","intervention":"eggs and toast","comparator":"cereal and milk with orange juice","outcome":"hunger reduction and ~180 kcal lower lunch intake","timeframe":"4 hours later","quantifiers":"~180 kcal less","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"In another study, eating eggs was associated with a 38% lower risk of excessive body fat and a 34% lower risk of central obesity, a known risk factor for metabolic syndrome.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"In [another studyTrusted Source](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31922457/), eating eggs was associated with a 38% lower risk of excessive body fat and a 34% lower risk of central obesity, or visceral fat around your abdomen area, which is a known risk factor for metabolic syndrome.","entailment":"The quoted sentence reports the observed associations between egg consumption and reduced risks of excess body fat and central obesity, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"risk of excessive body fat and central obesity","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"38% lower risk of excessive body fat, 34% lower risk of central obesity","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"It may be safe for people without high cholesterol to eat at least one egg daily.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"It [may be](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9143438/) safe for people without high cholesterol to eat at least one egg daily.","entailment":"The quote explicitly states that, for individuals without high cholesterol, consuming one egg per day may be safe, supporting the claim.","population":"people without high cholesterol","intervention":"eating at least one egg daily","comparator":"","outcome":"safety","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"conditional"},{"statement":"Eggs contain vitamin D, vitamin E, vitamin B6, calcium, and zinc, in addition to other beneficial nutrients like antioxidants.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Eggs [containTrusted Source](https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/fdc-app.html#/food-details/173424/nutrients) vitamin D, vitamin E, vitamin B6, calcium, and zinc, in addition to other beneficial nutrients like antioxidants.","entailment":"The quoted sentence lists specific vitamins and minerals present in eggs, confirming the claim about their nutrient content.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"presence of vitamins D, E, B6, calcium, zinc, antioxidants","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Eggs are high in fat and cholesterol.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Eggs [are highTrusted Source](https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/fdc-app.html#/food-details/173424/nutrients) in fat and cholesterol.","entailment":"The quote directly states that eggs contain high levels of fat and cholesterol, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"fat and cholesterol content","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"For some people who are more sensitive to the cholesterol in eggs, eating eggs daily may increase blood cholesterol.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"For some people who are more sensitive to the cholesterol in eggs, eating eggs daily [may increase](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9143438/) blood cholesterol.","entailment":"The quoted sentence indicates that, in cholesterol‑sensitive individuals, daily egg consumption may raise blood cholesterol levels, supporting the claim.","population":"cholesterol‑sensitive individuals","intervention":"daily egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"blood cholesterol increase","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"conditional"},{"statement":"Eggs are high in protein and contain beneficial nutrients, including choline and other vitamins.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Eggs are high in protein and contain beneficial nutrients, including choline and other vitamins.","entailment":"The quote lists protein, choline, and vitamins as beneficial nutrients present in eggs, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"protein, choline, vitamins","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Research is mixed on whether dietary cholesterol increases blood cholesterol for everyone.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"While eggs are high in cholesterol, research is mixed on whether dietary cholesterol increases blood cholesterol for everyone.","entailment":"The quoted sentence conveys uncertainty in the evidence regarding the effect of dietary cholesterol on blood cholesterol across all individuals, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"dietary cholesterol intake","comparator":"","outcome":"blood cholesterol levels","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"For most people, eating eggs daily is likely safe and a beneficial source of protein and vitamins.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"For most people, eating eggs daily is likely safe and a beneficial source of protein and vitamins.","entailment":"The quote asserts that, for the majority of individuals, daily egg consumption is probably safe and provides protein and vitamins, supporting the claim.","population":"most people","intervention":"daily egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"safety and nutrient benefit","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"conditional"},{"statement":"People with high cholesterol levels may want to talk with a doctor before drastically increasing their egg consumption.","claimType":"value","quote":"people with high cholesterol levels may want to talk with a doctor before drastically increasing their egg consumption.","entailment":"The quote advises individuals with high cholesterol to consult a doctor before significantly raising egg intake, supporting the recommendation claim.","population":"people with high cholesterol levels","intervention":"drastically increasing egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"medical consultation","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"normative"}]}
